title: ADR: Automated Spec Generation from Vague Engineering Tickets
context: Engineering tickets frequently enter the workflow as free-form, unstructured text lacking a problem statement, defined affected users, and testable acceptance criteria, forcing teams into repeated clarification cycles before work can begin.

Current baseline behavior: tickets are authored without enforced structure; recipients must manually interpret intent, infer scope, and derive acceptance criteria, resulting in inconsistent work items and rework.
Constraints: the capability must be usable by non-technical stakeholders without specialized training, and output must be consistent and reproducible for equivalent inputs.
decision: We will implement a spec generation capability that accepts free-form ticket text as input and produces a structured spec artifact containing at minimum a problem statement, affected users, and binary testable acceptance criteria, because automated structuring at the point of ticket authorship eliminates downstream clarification cycles, enforces a shared definition of 'structured spec', and makes output immediately actionable without manual reformatting by the requester.

inScope: Acceptance of free-form ticket text as the primary input signal
Generation of structured spec artifacts with required fields: problem statement, affected users, and acceptance criteria
Enforcement that each generated acceptance criterion is framed as a binary, observable check free of subjective judgment
Delivery of output in a format immediately usable as a work item without manual reformatting
outOfScope: Automatic implementation or resolution of the ticket contents
Integration with any specific ticketing system unless explicitly scoped in a follow-on decision

validationPlan.ac: Given a vague ticket input, the system produces a structured spec containing at minimum a problem statement, affected users, and acceptance criteria.
validationPlan.verification: Submit a deliberately vague, unstructured ticket and inspect the output artifact to confirm all three required fields are present and populated.

validationPlan.ac: Each generated acceptance criterion is binary and testable without subjective judgment.
validationPlan.verification: Review every AC in the generated spec against a binary framing rubric; flag any AC with subjective language.

validationPlan.ac: The structured spec is produced without requiring manual reformatting by the requester.
validationPlan.verification: Hand the raw capability output to a reviewer who did not author the input; confirm they can use it directly as an actionable work item.

risks.risk: Generated acceptance criteria contain ambiguous or non-binary language despite structured prompting
risks.trigger: Input tickets are highly sparse or domain-specific
risks.mitigation: Define a strict AC format template with explicit binary framing rules
risks.detection: Automated AC format validator on every output

risks.risk: Required spec fields are omitted or left empty when input is extremely minimal
risks.trigger: Input contains fewer contextual signals than the minimum needed
risks.mitigation: Field completeness gate that checks for minimum field population
risks.detection: Field presence check on every output artifact

risks.risk: Output inconsistency for equivalent inputs violates the reproducibility constraint
risks.trigger: Identical or near-identical ticket inputs produce structurally different specs
risks.mitigation: Apply deterministic generation settings and structured output schema enforcement
risks.detection: Run a fixed regression suite on each model or prompt version change

alternatives.name: Manual structured intake form
alternatives.reasonRejected: Requires requesters to fill out a structured form themselves, failing to address the root problem.

alternatives.name: Rule-based keyword extraction
alternatives.reasonRejected: Lacks semantic understanding needed to infer testable acceptance criteria from loosely stated intent.

openQuestions.question: What surface or interface will this capability be exposed through (e.g., web UI, CLI, API)?
What is the expected data source for incoming vague tickets (e.g., Jira, GitHub Issues, free-form text input)?
Is authentication or access control required to use the spec generation capability?
Is there an existing spec schema or template that output must conform to, or does one need to be defined?
